pick.rationale: UCLA enters this 7/10 matchup with a clear statistical advantage, ranking inside the top 30 in both KenPom and Torvik compared to UCF's standing in the mid-50s. The Bruins boast an elite top-20 offense that should consistently exploit a vulnerable Knights defense ranked outside the top 100 nationally. While UCLA is managing recent minor injuries to key contributors Tyler Bilodeau and Donovan Dent, both are expected to play, and the Bruins' significant edge in overall efficiency makes them a confident pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: UCLA vs UCF Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UCLA holds a significant advantage in both overall efficiency and offensive firepower, making them the clear favorite despite minor injury concerns.
evidence: UCLA ranks 24th in Torvik and 27th in KenPom, while UCF ranks 58th and 54th, respectively.
UCLA features a top-20 adjusted offense (17th in Torvik, 22nd in KenPom) that matches up very well against UCF's 114th-ranked Torvik defense.
UCLA's leading scorer Tyler Bilodeau (knee) and star guard Donovan Dent (leg) both suffered recent injuries but are expected to be available for the NCAA Tournament.
UCF is also dealing with multiple minor injuries from the Big 12 Tournament, neutralizing any potential health advantage.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026

Current game label: East 7/10
Round: Round of 64
